author: y4rr
content:
Decision: Architecture mismatch (depthwise vs regular conv) means true numerical parity vs torchvision is impossible for ConvNeXt and EfficientNet. Correct approach for Sprint V.1:

1. Use a TINY model variant (small channels: e.g. ConvNeXt with dims=[4,8,16,32], depths=[1,1,1,1]; EfficientNet with all out_ch/8) 
2. Set all weights to deterministic constant values (weight=const, bias=0) using load_state_dict  
3. Python script computes expected outputs using numpy, mirroring ferrotorch's exact computation (Conv2d as explicit correlation, LayerNorm, GELU, AdaptiveAvgPool, Linear)
4. fixtures_v_parity.json stores: all weight tensors, input, expected output
5. Rust test loads weights via load_state_dict, runs forward, asserts within F32_MATMUL=1e-3

This is stronger than shape-only tests (B.5.b) because it verifies numerical correctness of the forward pass against an independent computation.

Per-stage intermediate assertions: also record stem_output, stage0_output, etc. in fixture so test can localize divergence.
